meta.scene_summary: We are currently in a race to build the future while simultaneously breaking everything that currently works.

cooking.headline: AlphaFold's mastermind just moved to the Anthropic side of the fence.
cooking.summary: John Jumper is taking his protein-folding genius from Google to Anthropic for the next chapter. It's like watching the star player of a championship team transfer right before the playoffs start.
cooking.plain_english: John Jumper, a lead researcher known for his work on the AlphaFold protein-folding AI, has left Google DeepMind to join the artificial intelligence company Anthropic.

cooking.headline: Amazon decides a movie about Sam Altman is bad for business
cooking.summary: Amazon canceled a biopic about the OpenAI CEO right after signing a massive partnership with his company. It's hard to keep the PR narrative tight when your business partner is also the main character of a potentially unflattering documentary.
cooking.plain_english: Amazon has canceled plans to produce a film about Sam Altman following a new business agreement between Amazon and OpenAI.

cooking.headline: A new bill tries to stop the government from 'asking' nicely for censorship
cooking.summary: The government has been leaning on platforms to silence speech through polite suggestion rather than actual law. It is the digital equivalent of a landlord 'strongly recommending' you move out before your lease is actually up.
cooking.plain_english: A proposed bill aims to prevent government officials from pressuring social media companies to remove legal content.

cooking.headline: Open-source drone detection is the ultimate group project for national security
cooking.summary: A Lithuanian startup is crowdsourcing drone detection, proving that if you give the internet enough sensors, it will eventually build a defense system. It turns out the best way to track military hardware is to treat it like a public API that everyone can ping.
cooking.plain_english: A Lithuanian startup has created an open-source network that uses distributed sensors to identify and track Shahed-type drones.

cooked.headline: Companies are realizing that 'AI everything' isn't free.
cooked.summary: Businesses are finally looking at their cloud bills and realizing that the AI hype cycle is starting to hit their bottom line. It turns out that burning cash to generate hallucinations isn't a sustainable business model for everyone.
cooked.plain_english: Many companies are reducing their use of artificial intelligence tools because the high costs associated with these services are negatively impacting their budgets.

cooked.headline: The Arch User Repository is currently speedrunning a trust exercise
cooked.summary: Malicious actors are injecting malware into AUR packages, turning the community's favorite convenience feature into a liability. It turns out that 'installing from source' is only as safe as the stranger who last touched the repo.
cooked.plain_english: Users of the Arch User Repository have been targeted by malicious software hidden within community-contributed installation packages.

cooked.headline: Windows 11 update accidentally deletes your files and your peace of mind
cooked.summary: Microsoft pushed an update that nuked the Recycle Bin and broke OneDrive, essentially turning your OS into a digital scavenger hunt. It's comforting to know that even a trillion-dollar company still struggles with the 'delete' key.
cooked.plain_english: A recent Windows 11 software update caused technical issues including the malfunction of the Recycle Bin and synchronization errors with OneDrive.

cooked.headline: GPS is just a suggestion now
cooked.summary: Satellite data shows that signal tampering is happening on a massive scale, effectively turning our global navigation system into a game of 'guess where you actually are.' It's the digital equivalent of someone moving all the traffic cones while you're trying to merge onto the highway. More details in the original article.
cooked.plain_english: New satellite observations indicate that GPS signals are being manipulated and interfered with far more frequently and extensively than previously realized.

cooked.headline: Norway is ghosting AI in elementary schools
cooked.summary: Norway is basically putting AI in detention to save the curriculum from becoming a giant copy-paste operation. It turns out that teaching kids how to think is still harder than teaching a LLM how to hallucinate.
cooked.plain_english: The Norwegian government has implemented strict restrictions on the use of artificial intelligence tools in elementary schools.

cooked.headline: The UK wants to age-gate the internet with the precision of a junior dev's regex
cooked.summary: The UK government is trying to ban social media for under-16s, which is essentially asking a teenager to stop using a VPN. It's the legislative equivalent of trying to fix a memory leak by unplugging the server.
cooked.plain_english: The United Kingdom is proposing a law that would prohibit children under 16 from using social media platforms.
